key_themes: Strong coal and copper price tailwinds driving earnings and cash flow
Recent analyst upgrades (HSBC to Buy, Deutsche Bank raised PT to 610p)
Strategic deals in critical minerals (US-backed copper/cobalt stake), LNG contracts, aluminum recycling, and logistics
Exceptional share price performance (+114% 1-year, +35% YTD, near 52-week highs)
Resilient marketing/trading division providing upside amid volatility
summary: Glencore (GLEN.L) is seeing strongly positive sentiment after a massive rally, with the share price up approximately 114% over the past year and 35% YTD as of mid-April 2026, supported by high coal and copper prices.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/GLEN.L/)[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/GLEN.L/news/) Analysts have responded with upgrades, including HSBC moving to Buy (PT 620p) on coal strength and marketing outlook, and Deutsche Bank raising its target to 610p.[[3]](https://www.investing.com/equities/glencore-news)[[4]](https://x.com/lwsresearch/status/2044100036657545434) Key positive drivers include a US government-backed deal for a 40% stake in African copper-cobalt assets, new LNG agreements, aluminum recycling investments, and regaining ownership of logistics firm Access World. While some coverage questions if it's "too late" after such gains or highlights geopolitical risks (e.g. Iran-related shipping), investor and analyst discussions remain predominantly bullish on its diversified commodities exposure and trading capabilities.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/GLEN.L/)[[5]](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/GLEN.L/)
sources: 20+ recent news articles (Yahoo Finance, Reuters, Investing.com, MarketBeat), Dozens of X posts referencing $GLEN, GLEN.L or Glencore (sampled 15+ recent posts from March-April 2026)
